title: Two dials, not one
intro: So when DOES a quantity need more than one number per point? Meet a slab of strained silicon in a fixed electric field. Rotate the slab and probe the current that flows. If the response were a plain number, the current would always follow the field. Watch what actually happens at a few different angles.
explain: The current refuses to follow E unless the slab's strain axis lines up with it — the response depends on DIRECTION, so one number per point is not enough. Conductivity here is a rank-2 tensor σ̿ (a 2×2 matrix): J = σ̿·E, two indices, 'how much of a push along x ends up as flow along y'. THAT is what tensors are for. Potential never needs this — V answers a question with no direction in it. Rank (how many indices) and domain dimension (1D/2D/3D) are independent dials, and confusing them is the most common tensor myth. Anchor: strain engineering has boosted silicon mobility in production CMOS since the 90 nm node (2003).

choices: The current always follows the field
The current tilts toward the strained axis

prediction.prompt: Rotate the slab 45° to the field. What does the current do?
